Product Name: It Could Happen to You (TTBB) (arr. Cokeroft)
Product Description: TTBB, for male/low voices | Arranged by Gene Cokeroft | Available in USA and Canada This arrangement was performed by Upshot! quartet at the 2020 Senior Quartet Contest held in Jacksonville, FL. The quartet placed 6th in their very first contest together and featured Bruce Cokeroft on tenor, son of the late Gene Cokeroft (arranger), and barbershop legend Jack Slamka on bass. Upshot! was the last quartet Slamka sang with before his untimely passing in the fall of 2020 due to complications of COVID-19. The Barbershop Harmony Society is pleased to publish this arrangement in honor of Jack Slamka, his lifelong dedication to the barbershop artform, and his indelible legacy.

TTBB, arranged by the Barbershop Harmony Society (SPEBSQSA) | This traditional song first appeared in the Society’s Songs for Men Book 6, released to the membership in 1953. there were twelve such song books from 1948 through 1961, representing most of the official published arrangements of barbershop harmony available from SPEBSQSA. After reviewing 160 songs in the twelve books, a consensus of opinion indicated some of the songs should be made available again—not only for old times’ sake, but because many new barbershoppers have never sung some of the “hits” of years past. This arrangement has been revised a bit from the way Tom Grove transcribed it “as sung by The Volunteers of Baltimore, MD” in the early 1950s.   |  Thanks to Society Volunteer Mike Rosen for converting this arrangement from the original octavo format!
